_notes: Source: AU Core Encounter (Australian realm). Target: US Core Encounter (US realm). Both mark status, class, subject(min=1), participant, participant.type, participant.individual, period, reasonCode, reasonReference, location, location.location, serviceProvider as MS. Key differences: US Core adds mandatory identifier with sub-element constraints (system/value min=1); US Core makes type mandatory (min=1) with us-core-encounter-type binding; AU Core has serviceType MS with preferred AU VS while US Core doesn't constrain serviceType; US Core adds participant.period MS absent in AU Core; participant.individual reference targets differ (AU vs US profiles); reasonReference targets differ; US Core adds hospitalization and hospitalization.dischargeDisposition MS; location.location AU profile constraint absent in US Core; serviceProvider references differ (au-core vs us-core).

drifts.description: Extension slicing removed in target: source AU Core defines open slicing on Encounter.extension with named slice associatedHealthcareService; US Core Encounter has no extension slicing.
Extension associatedHealthcareService removed in target: AU Core defines Encounter.extension:associatedHealthcareService (optional, no MS); US Core Encounter has no such extension slice.
identifier MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds identifier[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain identifier. MS added on optional element.

identifier.system min cardinality strictly increased: target adds identifier.system[min=1, max=1, mustSupport=true]; source has no constraint on identifier.system (effective min=0). New mandatory constraint added in target.

identifier.value min cardinality strictly increased: target adds identifier.value[min=1, max=1, mustSupport=true]; source has no constraint on identifier.value (effective min=0). New mandatory constraint added in target.

class binding changed: source extensible(http://terminology.hl7.org.au/ValueSet/v3-ActEncounterCode-extended) [AU extended encounter code VS] → target extensible(http://terminology.hl7.org/ValueSet/v3-ActEncounterCode) [R4 base encounter code VS]. Same binding strength (extensible), AU-extended VS replaced by base VS.
Encounter.type min cardinality strictly increased: target adds type[min=1, mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain type (effective min=0 from R4 base). Min increased 0→1.

Encounter.type binding changed: source preferred(https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/ValueSet/encounter-type-1) [AU encounter type VS] → target extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-encounter-type) [US Core encounter type VS]. Binding strength weakened from preferred to extensible (AU base preferred → US extensible) and AU-specific VS replaced.

Encounter.type binding added in target: US Core adds extensible(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-encounter-type); source AU Core has no binding on type in its differential (inherits R4 example binding). Binding tightened from R4 example to extensible with US realm-specific VS.
serviceType MustSupport removed on optional element: source mustSupport=true; target US Core does not constrain serviceType. MS removed on optional element.

serviceType binding changed: source preferred(https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/ValueSet/service-type-1) [AU service type VS] → target example(http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/service-type) [R4 base VS]. Binding strength weakened from preferred to example and AU-specific VS replaced.

subject reference target changed: source constrains to au-core-patient (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/core/StructureDefinition/au-core-patient) → target constrains to us-core-patient (http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-patient). Different realm-specific patient profiles.

participant.period MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds participant.period[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain participant.period. MS added on optional element.

participant.individual reference targets changed: source [au-core-practitioner, au-core-practitionerrole, au-relatedperson] → target [us-core-practitioner, us-core-practitionerrole, us-core-relatedperson]. AU realm-specific profiles replaced by US realm-specific profiles.
participant.individual type-level MS added in target: US Core marks us-core-practitioner reference as type-level MustSupport.

reasonReference reference targets changed: source [au-core-condition, Observation, au-core-procedure] → target [us-core-condition-problems-health-concerns, us-core-condition-encounter-diagnosis, us-core-procedure, Observation, ImmunizationRecommendation]. AU profiles replaced by US profiles; ImmunizationRecommendation added as new target in US Core.
reasonReference type-level MS added in target: US Core marks us-core-condition-problems-health-concerns and us-core-condition-encounter-diagnosis references as type-level MustSupport.
hospitalization MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds hospitalization[mustSupport=true]; source AU Core does not constrain hospitalization. MS added on optional element.

hospitalization.dischargeDisposition MustSupport added on optional element in target: US Core adds dischargeDisposition[mustSupport=true]; source does not constrain this element. MS added on optional element.

hospitalization.dischargeDisposition binding changed: source extensible(https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/ValueSet/separation-mode-1) [AU separation mode VS] → target preferred(http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/ValueSet/us-core-discharge-disposition) [US Core discharge disposition VS]. Binding strength changed from extensible to preferred and AU-specific VS replaced by US Core VS.

location.location AU profile constraint removed in target: source constrains to au-core-location (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/core/StructureDefinition/au-core-location); target has mustSupport=true but no profile constraint (allows base R4 Location). AU realm-specific profile reference removed.

serviceProvider reference target changed: source constrains to au-core-organization (http://hl7.org.au/fhir/core/StructureDefinition/au-core-organization) → target constrains to us-core-organization (http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-organization). Different realm-specific organization profiles.
